texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Release Notes
texts.text: Release Notes

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This manual describes enhancements and fixes included in the combined build of Clinical Reminders patch PXRM*2*6 - MH Integration and GMTS*2.7*77 - Mental Health Components.
texts.text: This manual describes enhancements and fixes included in the combined build of Clinical Reminders patch PXRM*2*6 - MH Integration and GMTS*2.7*77 - Mental Health Components.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Clinical Reminders PXRM*2*6 Documentation
texts.text: Clinical Reminders PXRM*2*6 Documentation

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Documentation
texts.text: Documentation

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Documentation File name
texts.text: Documentation File name

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Patch PXRM*2*6 contains modifications to integrate the Clinical Reminders package with the new version of the Mental Package called MHA3.  The Clinical Reminders package will support use of new mental health surveys, instruments, and forms for clinical collection, reminder evaluation, patient list building and reporting. These modifications will be distributed at the same time as YS*5.01*85.
texts.text: Patch PXRM*2*6 contains modifications to integrate the Clinical Reminders package with the new version of the Mental Package called MHA3.  The Clinical Reminders package will support use of new mental health surveys, instruments, and forms for clinical collection, reminder evaluation, patient list building and reporting. These modifications will be distributed at the same time as YS*5.01*85.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This functionality is needed so that Clinical Reminders can be used to help sites meet the Performance Measure requirements related to a standardized set of Mental Health Instruments that will be available in the YS*5.01*85 patch.  The standardized instruments are AUDC, BDI2, PHQ-2, PHQ9, and PC-PTSD.
texts.text: This functionality is needed so that Clinical Reminders can be used to help sites meet the Performance Measure requirements related to a standardized set of Mental Health Instruments that will be available in the YS*5.01*85 patch.  The standardized instruments are AUDC, BDI2, PHQ-2, PHQ9, and PC-PTSD.

texts.orig: NOTE: In order to use all of the dialog functionality available with MHA3 and PXRM*2*6, Version 27 of the CPRS GUI will need to be installed. This version isn’t scheduled for release until later in the year.
texts.text: NOTE: In order to use all of the dialog functionality available with MHA3 and PXRM*2*6, Version 27 of the CPRS GUI will need to be installed. This version isn’t scheduled for release until later in the year.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Version 27 adds the ability to use a Mental Health DLL when a MH test is invoked from a reminder dialog. (YS_MHA.DLL is included with YS*5.01*85 and must be installed in the folder \Program Files\vista\ Common Files on all workstations.)
texts.text: Version 27 adds the ability to use a Mental Health DLL when a MH test is invoked from a reminder dialog. (YS_MHA.DLL is included with YS*5.01*85 and must be installed in the folder \Program Files\vista\ Common Files on all workstations.)

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Reminder Definitions/Terms
texts.text: Reminder Definitions/Terms

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: To aid sites in making the conversion of Clinical Reminders to use MHA3, the post-init will convert all existing mental health findings to their MHA3 equivalent and MH SCALE values to the appropriate MHA3 scale. If the field MH SCALE is null, then the score for the first scale returned by MHA3 will be displayed in the Clinical Maintenance output.
texts.text: To aid sites in making the conversion of Clinical Reminders to use MHA3, the post-init will convert all existing mental health findings to their MHA3 equivalent and MH SCALE values to the appropriate MHA3 scale. If the field MH SCALE is null, then the score for the first scale returned by MHA3 will be displayed in the Clinical Maintenance output.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: When MH SCALE has a value, it will set the value of V for use in a Condition. In other words, V will be the score according to the scale stored in MH SCALE. Another change is that score is now returned as raw score^transformed score. Thus, if your Condition uses the raw score, you will use +V or $P(V,U,1) and if it uses the transformed score, use $P(V,U,2). The post-init will convert V to +V in all existing national Conditions for MH findings.
texts.text: When MH SCALE has a value, it will set the value of V for use in a Condition. In other words, V will be the score according to the scale stored in MH SCALE. Another change is that score is now returned as raw score^transformed score. Thus, if your Condition uses the raw score, you will use +V or $P(V,U,1) and if it uses the transformed score, use $P(V,U,2). The post-init will convert V to +V in all existing national Conditions for MH findings.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The entire set of scores has been made into a CSUB item in patch PXRM*2*6, so that any score or combination of scores can be used in a Condition. For example, the MH Test AUIR has scales 279 through 329; if you want to use the raw score for scale 300, then you can use +V(“S”,300).
texts.text: The entire set of scores has been made into a CSUB item in patch PXRM*2*6, so that any score or combination of scores can be used in a Condition. For example, the MH Test AUIR has scales 279 through 329; if you want to use the raw score for scale 300, then you can use +V(“S”,300).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: NOTE:  Typing a “?” at the MH SCALE prompt will give you a list of all the scales available for the MH Test you have selected. It shows you both the scale number and the scale name. Because the scale number is much easier to use, it is the way we refer to scales in reminder definitions and terms.
texts.text: NOTE:  Typing a “?” at the MH SCALE prompt will give you a list of all the scales available for the MH Test you have selected. It shows you both the scale number and the scale name. Because the scale number is much easier to use, it is the way we refer to scales in reminder definitions and terms.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Responses to individual questions can also be used in a Condition. For example, if you want to test the response to question number 7, you would use V(“R”,7).
texts.text: Responses to individual questions can also be used in a Condition. For example, if you want to test the response to question number 7, you would use V(“R”,7).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: NOTE
texts.text: NOTE

texts.label: text
texts.orig: No national reminder definitions use MH findings, but those national terms that use MH findings will have correct values set for MH SCALE, and where applicable, the Condition will be updated also. These terms will be redistributed in patch PXRM*2*6.
texts.text: No national reminder definitions use MH findings, but those national terms that use MH findings will have correct values set for MH SCALE, and where applicable, the Condition will be updated also. These terms will be redistributed in patch PXRM*2*6.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: A new function finding function, NUMERIC, was added.
texts.text: A new function finding function, NUMERIC, was added.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: DESCRIPTION: The NUMERIC function returns the first numeric portion of any of the "CSUB" values for a finding. For example, if the COMMENT field of a health factor contains a numerical value, this function can be used to test it. If you want to check to see if the first numeric portion in the COMMENT field of finding 1 occurrence 1 is greater than 2, then the function finding would be:
texts.text: DESCRIPTION: The NUMERIC function returns the first numeric portion of any of the "CSUB" values for a finding. For example, if the COMMENT field of a health factor contains a numerical value, this function can be used to test it. If you want to check to see if the first numeric portion in the COMMENT field of finding 1 occurrence 1 is greater than 2, then the function finding would be:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: NUMERIC(1,1,"COMMENTS")>2
texts.text: NUMERIC(1,1,"COMMENTS")>2

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Note: OCCURRENCE COUNT for the finding must be equal to or greater than the occurrence(s) you want to use.
texts.text: Note: OCCURRENCE COUNT for the finding must be equal to or greater than the occurrence(s) you want to use.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The primary provider DUZ was added to the data returned for a Visit file entry. If there is no primary provider, the value will be null. TYPE, HOSPITAL LOCATION, STOP CODE, and ENTERED BY were also added to the data returned for a Vitals entry.
texts.text: The primary provider DUZ was added to the data returned for a Visit file entry. If there is no primary provider, the value will be null. TYPE, HOSPITAL LOCATION, STOP CODE, and ENTERED BY were also added to the data returned for a Vitals entry.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Clinical Reminders normally treats partial dates as follows: if the day is missing, it is assumed to be the first; if the month is missing, it is assumed to be January. When a Custom Date Due was used, this convention was not being followed. The code was changed to follow this convention.
texts.text: Clinical Reminders normally treats partial dates as follows: if the day is missing, it is assumed to be the first; if the month is missing, it is assumed to be January. When a Custom Date Due was used, this convention was not being followed. The code was changed to follow this convention.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: A typo in error message text for Vitals findings was corrected. The name of the global was GMRV(120.5; it was corrected to GMR(120.5.
texts.text: A typo in error message text for Vitals findings was corrected. The name of the global was GMRV(120.5; it was corrected to GMR(120.5.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Processing of Location List findings was originally based on the AET Visit file index which includes Encounter Type. Encounter Type is not a required field; consequently any Visit file entries that do not have an Encounter Type will not be in this index and would be missed. The code was changed to use the AA index so no entries will be missed. As an added bonus, it turned out that using AA is faster than AET.
texts.text: Processing of Location List findings was originally based on the AET Visit file index which includes Encounter Type. Encounter Type is not a required field; consequently any Visit file entries that do not have an Encounter Type will not be in this index and would be missed. The code was changed to use the AA index so no entries will be missed. As an added bonus, it turned out that using AA is faster than AET.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: When BEGINNING DATE/TIME and ENDING DATE/TIME were input as FileMan dates including time, the time was not being displayed in reminder inquiry. This was corrected.
texts.text: When BEGINNING DATE/TIME and ENDING DATE/TIME were input as FileMan dates including time, the time was not being displayed in reminder inquiry. This was corrected.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The MHV output for non-VA meds was producing an error when there was no stop date. This was corrected.
texts.text: The MHV output for non-VA meds was producing an error when there was no stop date. This was corrected.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: There was a bug when editing terms in national reminder definitions. A list of terms to edit is presented and the user selects which term to edit. If the user selected terms 3, 5, and 7, they would actually get terms 1, 2, and 3 to edit. This was corrected.
texts.text: There was a bug when editing terms in national reminder definitions. A list of terms to edit is presented and the user selects which term to edit. If the user selected terms 3, 5, and 7, they would actually get terms 1, 2, and 3 to edit. This was corrected.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: If a term contained multiple drug findings, the name of the most recent drug was being displayed for all the findings, even though the rest of the information such as start date and days supply was correct. The code was changed so that the correct drug name is displayed for each finding.
texts.text: If a term contained multiple drug findings, the name of the most recent drug was being displayed for all the findings, even though the rest of the information such as start date and days supply was correct. The code was changed so that the correct drug name is displayed for each finding.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: For drug class or VA Generic findings that contain many drugs, it is possible that different drugs on the list may have the same pharmacy orderable item. When this was the case and non-VA meds were included in the search, multiple instances of the same non-VA med were being put on the list. To prevent this, a check was added to make sure the same instance was not already on the list before adding it.
texts.text: For drug class or VA Generic findings that contain many drugs, it is possible that different drugs on the list may have the same pharmacy orderable item. When this was the case and non-VA meds were included in the search, multiple instances of the same non-VA med were being put on the list. To prevent this, a check was added to make sure the same instance was not already on the list before adding it.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: An undefined error associated with the status list when adding a reminder taxonomy as a finding item to either a reminder definition or a reminder term was fixed. To test this, the user would need to create a taxonomy that contains both Radiology CPT Codes and ICD9 Codes and the Patient Data Source is set to “All”. Remedy #168830 and #177389.
texts.text: An undefined error associated with the status list when adding a reminder taxonomy as a finding item to either a reminder definition or a reminder term was fixed. To test this, the user would need to create a taxonomy that contains both Radiology CPT Codes and ICD9 Codes and the Patient Data Source is set to “All”. Remedy #168830 and #177389.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Building the drug status list was changed to use a new pharmacy encapsulation API instead of FileMan calls.
texts.text: Building the drug status list was changed to use a new pharmacy encapsulation API instead of FileMan calls.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Plus and minus were inadvertently left out of the list of permissible operators in Function Findings; they were added to the list.
texts.text: Plus and minus were inadvertently left out of the list of permissible operators in Function Findings; they were added to the list.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The selection display in reminder definition edit was changed to show if a definition has been inactivated.
texts.text: The selection display in reminder definition edit was changed to show if a definition has been inactivated.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: A site had a problem with a reminder because their default resolution logic (built from USE IN RESOLUTION LOGIC fields on the findings) allowed the reminder to be resolved solely by a function finding. Checking during reminder definition edit was added that will notify the user when this situation occurs.
texts.text: A site had a problem with a reminder because their default resolution logic (built from USE IN RESOLUTION LOGIC fields on the findings) allowed the reminder to be resolved solely by a function finding. Checking during reminder definition edit was added that will notify the user when this situation occurs.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The definition and term inquiries displayed the old name of the field USE STATUS/COND IN SEARCH. These were corrected to use the new name.
texts.text: The definition and term inquiries displayed the old name of the field USE STATUS/COND IN SEARCH. These were corrected to use the new name.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: PXRM*2*6 adds three new fields to the DD for 801.41. These fields will be used by Result Group/Elements and Dialog Elements.
texts.text: PXRM*2*6 adds three new fields to the DD for 801.41. These fields will be used by Result Group/Elements and Dialog Elements.

texts.text: ^PXRMD(801.41,D0,51,0)=^801.41121P^^  (#121) RESULT GROUP SEQUENCE

texts.text: ^PXRMD(801.41,D0,51,D1,0)= (#.01) RESULT GROUP SEQUENCE [1P:801.41] ^

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: MH Test is defined when creating/editing a Result Group. As of PXRM*2*6, all Result Groups must be mapped to a MH Test.
texts.text: MH Test is defined when creating/editing a Result Group. As of PXRM*2*6, all Result Groups must be mapped to a MH Test.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: MH Scale is defined when creating/editing a Result Group. The list of possible scales is based on the MH Test defined in field #119. As of PXRM*2*6, all Result Groups need to be mapped to a MH Scale.
texts.text: MH Scale is defined when creating/editing a Result Group. The list of possible scales is based on the MH Test defined in field #119. As of PXRM*2*6, all Result Groups need to be mapped to a MH Scale.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Result Group Sequence is replacing the Result Group/Element field used when creating/editing a Dialog Element for a MH Test. With the new MH DLL in CPRS 27, it is now possible to evaluate multiple scores for each MH Test that contains multiple scales. In the past, we could only evaluate one score per test. With PXRM*2*6, only a Result Group can be assigned to a Dialog Element.
texts.text: Result Group Sequence is replacing the Result Group/Element field used when creating/editing a Dialog Element for a MH Test. With the new MH DLL in CPRS 27, it is now possible to evaluate multiple scores for each MH Test that contains multiple scales. In the past, we could only evaluate one score per test. With PXRM*2*6, only a Result Group can be assigned to a Dialog Element.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Field (#55) RESULT GROUP/ELEMENT [15P:801.41] will be deleted from the 801.41 DD.
texts.text: Field (#55) RESULT GROUP/ELEMENT [15P:801.41] will be deleted from the 801.41 DD.

texts.orig: Maximum Number of MH Questions has been added as a new field to file 800.
texts.text: Maximum Number of MH Questions has been added as a new field to file 800.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: All the National Result Groups and Result Elements will be re-released with PXRM*2*6. These have been updated to use the correct MH Test and the MH Scale.
texts.text: All the National Result Groups and Result Elements will be re-released with PXRM*2*6. These have been updated to use the correct MH Test and the MH Scale.

texts.label: text

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The PXRM BDI RESULT GROUP has been marked disabled. Sites should use the PXRM BDI II RESULT GROUP instead of this result group.
texts.text: The PXRM BDI RESULT GROUP has been marked disabled. Sites should use the PXRM BDI II RESULT GROUP instead of this result group.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: NOTE: The MH instrument BDI is being discontinued. The Beck Depression Inventory is an instrument in the Mental Health Assistant that has long been used for evaluating and monitoring depression.  For several years, MHA carried both the original version (BDI) and a newer, enhanced version (BDI2).  With the release of patch YS*5.01*85, the BDI will be inactivated, as the BDI2 is now the preferred version of this instrument. During the pre-init, any dialog elements using BDI will be changed to use BDI2.
texts.text: NOTE: The MH instrument BDI is being discontinued. The Beck Depression Inventory is an instrument in the Mental Health Assistant that has long been used for evaluating and monitoring depression.  For several years, MHA carried both the original version (BDI) and a newer, enhanced version (BDI2).  With the release of patch YS*5.01*85, the BDI will be inactivated, as the BDI2 is now the preferred version of this instrument. During the pre-init, any dialog elements using BDI will be changed to use BDI2.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: National Result Groups assigned to a dialog element will be moved to the new multiple "RESULT GROUP SEQUENCE" if the test assigned to the Result Group matches the MH Finding Item in the element. These items will be stored as the first position. Local Result Groups will not be moved because of the lack of MH Tests defined for the Result Groups. Any Result Group that is not moved should be displayed in a MailMan message stating the name of the Result Group and the Element.
texts.text: National Result Groups assigned to a dialog element will be moved to the new multiple "RESULT GROUP SEQUENCE" if the test assigned to the Result Group matches the MH Finding Item in the element. These items will be stored as the first position. Local Result Groups will not be moved because of the lack of MH Tests defined for the Result Groups. Any Result Group that is not moved should be displayed in a MailMan message stating the name of the Result Group and the Element.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Result Element Editor
texts.text: Result Element Editor

texts.label: text
texts.orig: A new field was added to the Result Group Editor “Informational Text”; this field allows the sites to add text to a pop-up warning in CPRS. (CPRS 27 and the MH DLL are needed to support this functionality). When CPRS is evaluating the Result Element progress note text, if the Result Element is true, the Informational Text defined in the Result Element will be returned to CPRS 27.
texts.text: A new field was added to the Result Group Editor “Informational Text”; this field allows the sites to add text to a pop-up warning in CPRS. (CPRS 27 and the MH DLL are needed to support this functionality). When CPRS is evaluating the Result Element progress note text, if the Result Element is true, the Informational Text defined in the Result Element will be returned to CPRS 27.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Result Group Editor
texts.text: Result Group Editor

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In the Result Group Editor, sites will be able to disable a Result Group. Sites will also be able to assign the MH Test and the MH Scale to the Result Group. Both an MH Test and a MH Scale are required before the Result Group can be used in CPRS. A disabled Result Group will not be used in CPRS.
texts.text: In the Result Group Editor, sites will be able to disable a Result Group. Sites will also be able to assign the MH Test and the MH Scale to the Result Group. Both an MH Test and a MH Scale are required before the Result Group can be used in CPRS. A disabled Result Group will not be used in CPRS.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Several enhancements were made to result group editing. Result groups are screened to make sure they match the MH test. If the MH test is changed, any existing result groups are checked and if they do not match the MH finding, they are deleted.
texts.text: Several enhancements were made to result group editing. Result groups are screened to make sure they match the MH test. If the MH test is changed, any existing result groups are checked and if they do not match the MH finding, they are deleted.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Dialog Element Editor
texts.text: Dialog Element Editor

texts.label: text
texts.orig: When defining an MH finding item in a dialog element, the user will be able to assign multiple result groups to a dialog element. This is done to support the enhanced functionality of the MH DLL in CPRS 27. The list of Result Groups should be limited to Result Groups that have the same MH test as the MH finding Item, an MH Scale defined, and the Result Group is not disabled. When an MH test is defined in a dialog element, a check will be done to see if the test requires a license. If the test requires a license, a message will be displayed to the user stating “The question text will not appear in the progress note.”
texts.text: When defining an MH finding item in a dialog element, the user will be able to assign multiple result groups to a dialog element. This is done to support the enhanced functionality of the MH DLL in CPRS 27. The list of Result Groups should be limited to Result Groups that have the same MH test as the MH finding Item, an MH Scale defined, and the Result Group is not disabled. When an MH test is defined in a dialog element, a check will be done to see if the test requires a license. If the test requires a license, a message will be displayed to the user stating “The question text will not appear in the progress note.”

texts.label: text
texts.orig: New Dialog Option
texts.text: New Dialog Option

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: A new option, Edit Number of MH Questions, has been added to the Reminder Parameters menu. This option allows the site to set the maximum number of questions an MH test can have and be administered via a Reminder Dialog. The default value when PXRM*2*6 is installed is 35. The user will not be able to select a MH test with a number of questions that exceeds the value defined in this option.
texts.text: A new option, Edit Number of MH Questions, has been added to the Reminder Parameters menu. This option allows the site to set the maximum number of questions an MH test can have and be administered via a Reminder Dialog. The default value when PXRM*2*6 is installed is 35. The user will not be able to select a MH test with a number of questions that exceeds the value defined in this option.

texts.text: YS_MHA.DLL is a new tool included with YS*5.01*85 that provides an interface to Clinical Reminders functions in CPRS27. As described in the YS*5.01*85 Installation Guide, this DLL must be deployed to \Program Files\vista\ Common Files.

The question and answer text for the progress note, along with the score and scale for each MH test, will be returned by the MH DLL.

texts.orig: CPRS 26 has additional checks to avoid forcing the user to answer all the questions in the MH test if the test is considered resolved without answering all of the questions. This requires installation of PXRM*2*6 and YS*5.01*85 to work.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: CPRS 26 and PXRM*2*6
texts.text: CPRS 26 and PXRM*2*6

texts.label: text
texts.orig: . PXRM*2*6 can contain multiple Result Groups; however, CPRS 26 is only expecting one Result Group per element. If the dialog element contains more than one dialog result group in the Result Group Sequence Multiple, only the first Result Group in the multiple will be sent to CPRS 26. The informational message can be defined in the Result Element; however, the Informational message will not display in CPRS 26. The Reminder Manager will be able to set up a dialog with MH Tests that do not work in CPRS 26. An error message "Error encountered loading MH Test Name" will be displayed in CPRS. The MH Test BOMC is an example of a test that can be defined in PXRM*2*6, but will not function correctly until CPRS 27 and the MH DLL.
texts.text: . PXRM*2*6 can contain multiple Result Groups; however, CPRS 26 is only expecting one Result Group per element. If the dialog element contains more than one dialog result group in the Result Group Sequence Multiple, only the first Result Group in the multiple will be sent to CPRS 26. The informational message can be defined in the Result Element; however, the Informational message will not display in CPRS 26. The Reminder Manager will be able to set up a dialog with MH Tests that do not work in CPRS 26. An error message "Error encountered loading MH Test Name" will be displayed in CPRS. The MH Test BOMC is an example of a test that can be defined in PXRM*2*6, but will not function correctly until CPRS 27 and the MH DLL.

texts.text: CPRS 27 and PXRM*2*6 are installed, but the MH DLL is not running.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: CPRS 27 will be able to handle a list of Result Groups. However, the original Result Group evaluation code will not be able to support dialog elements for Result Groups. The Result Group evaluation code will take the first Result Group in the list and will process this Result Group as the only Result Group for the dialog element. The informational message can be defined in the Result Element; however, the Informational message will not display in CPRS 26. The Reminder Manager will be able to set up a dialog containing MH Tests that do not work in CPRS 26. An error message "Error encountered loading MH Test Name" will be displayed in CPRS. The MH Test BOMC is an example of a test that can be defined in PXRM*2*6, but will not function correctly until CPRS 27 and the MH DLL.
texts.text: CPRS 27 will be able to handle a list of Result Groups. However, the original Result Group evaluation code will not be able to support dialog elements for Result Groups. The Result Group evaluation code will take the first Result Group in the list and will process this Result Group as the only Result Group for the dialog element. The informational message can be defined in the Result Element; however, the Informational message will not display in CPRS 26. The Reminder Manager will be able to set up a dialog containing MH Tests that do not work in CPRS 26. An error message "Error encountered loading MH Test Name" will be displayed in CPRS. The MH Test BOMC is an example of a test that can be defined in PXRM*2*6, but will not function correctly until CPRS 27 and the MH DLL.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: . Once CPRS 27 is released and the MH DLL is running, everything is in place to support the new functionality. Each Result Group per dialog element will be evaluated against the score(s) for each scale returned from the DLL. The Informational Message will appear in CPRS 27, and MH Tests such as the BOMC will work with CPRS 27 and the MH DLL.

Each Result Group per dialog element will be evaluated against the score(s) for each scale returned from the DLL. The Informational Message will appear in CPRS 27, and MH Tests such as the BOMC will work with CPRS 27 and the MH DLL.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Reminder Exchange
texts.text: Reminder Exchange

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: A problem in Reminder Exchange was reported where a definition would not install and it was giving an error in a FileMan routine. The problem was tracked to the input screen on field 1.4 in file #811.9; on examination of the data dictionary the input screen was corrupted. The solution was to remove the screen.
texts.text: A problem in Reminder Exchange was reported where a definition would not install and it was giving an error in a FileMan routine. The problem was tracked to the input screen on field 1.4 in file #811.9; on examination of the data dictionary the input screen was corrupted. The solution was to remove the screen.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: A problem with replacement elements/group not installing if a reminder dialog contains branching logic was fixed.
texts.text: A problem with replacement elements/group not installing if a reminder dialog contains branching logic was fixed.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: A problem with a new sponsor entry not installing when the entry is only defined as a sub-item in a reminder dialog was fixed. Remedy #169122.
texts.text: A problem with a new sponsor entry not installing when the entry is only defined as a sub-item in a reminder dialog was fixed. Remedy #169122.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: A problem with Reminder Exchange not allowing sites to install Result Group/Elements was fixed. Result Group/Elements will be handled similarly to how dialog prompts are handled.
texts.text: A problem with Reminder Exchange not allowing sites to install Result Group/Elements was fixed. Result Group/Elements will be handled similarly to how dialog prompts are handled.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: An “X” in the “Exists” column means that an entry with an identical name already exists on the system. Even though the names are identical, the contents of the packed component could be different than what is on the system. For example, a new code could have been added to a taxonomy or new finding added to a term. The use of checksums was added to Reminder Exchange to provide a way to determine if packed and installed components with identical names also have identical contents. When a component is selected for installation and it already exists on the system, the checksum for the installed version will be computed and compared with the checksum of the packed version. If the checksums match, then the component will automatically be skipped. From now on, when components are packed, their checksums will be stored. Checksums will be calculated for components that were packed before this change was made so the checksum test will work in all cases.
texts.text: An “X” in the “Exists” column means that an entry with an identical name already exists on the system. Even though the names are identical, the contents of the packed component could be different than what is on the system. For example, a new code could have been added to a taxonomy or new finding added to a term. The use of checksums was added to Reminder Exchange to provide a way to determine if packed and installed components with identical names also have identical contents. When a component is selected for installation and it already exists on the system, the checksum for the installed version will be computed and compared with the checksum of the packed version. If the checksums match, then the component will automatically be skipped. From now on, when components are packed, their checksums will be stored. Checksums will be calculated for components that were packed before this change was made so the checksum test will work in all cases.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Reminder Exchange was changed so that it will not attempt to reinstall a dialog element that has already been installed. When a dialog element was used more than once in the dialog, an attempt was made to install it again when it was encountered a second time on the list of dialog elements. Because of the key, the duplicate entry was not installed and an error message was displayed. The dialog was not corrupted –  the only problem was the display of unnecessary error messages.
texts.text: Reminder Exchange was changed so that it will not attempt to reinstall a dialog element that has already been installed. When a dialog element was used more than once in the dialog, an attempt was made to install it again when it was encountered a second time on the list of dialog elements. Because of the key, the duplicate entry was not installed and an error message was displayed. The dialog was not corrupted –  the only problem was the display of unnecessary error messages.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Reminder Extracts and Patient Lists
texts.text: Reminder Extracts and Patient Lists

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The format of the “Scheduled to Run:” date in Extract Summary was changed from a FileMan date to an external date.
texts.text: The format of the “Scheduled to Run:” date in Extract Summary was changed from a FileMan date to an external date.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The List Manager Extract Summary display was changed to use the reminder print name if it exists. If it does not exist, then the .01 is used. This makes the name display in Extracts consistent with Clinical Maintenance and Reminder Reports.
texts.text: The List Manager Extract Summary display was changed to use the reminder print name if it exists. If it does not exist, then the .01 is used. This makes the name display in Extracts consistent with Clinical Maintenance and Reminder Reports.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Previously, when a patient list was created, the first step was to initialize a stub in the Patient List file; the stub contained only the NAME and the CLASS. If there was an error populating the list and the stub was left, then only someone holding the PXRM MANAGER key could delete it. The stub initialization was changed so that it also inserts the CREATOR and sets the initial TYPE to be public. This makes it possible for the person who created the list to delete it if an error occurs that prevents normal completion of the list-building process.
texts.text: Previously, when a patient list was created, the first step was to initialize a stub in the Patient List file; the stub contained only the NAME and the CLASS. If there was an error populating the list and the stub was left, then only someone holding the PXRM MANAGER key could delete it. The stub initialization was changed so that it also inserts the CREATOR and sets the initial TYPE to be public. This makes it possible for the person who created the list to delete it if an error occurs that prevents normal completion of the list-building process.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: If a patient is on a patient list and for some reason the patient is later deleted from the Patient File, then running a Patient Demographic Report or a Health Summary would generate an error. Code was added to handle this problem.
texts.text: If a patient is on a patient list and for some reason the patient is later deleted from the Patient File, then running a Patient Demographic Report or a Health Summary would generate an error. Code was added to handle this problem.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Dates shown in patient list creation documentation did not always match those displayed by the rule set test action. To correct this, a new routine was created, to be used for all patient list date calculation. Some of the basic date utility functions used throughout Clinical Reminders were optimized to get better performance.
texts.text: Dates shown in patient list creation documentation did not always match those displayed by the rule set test action. To correct this, a new routine was created, to be used for all patient list date calculation. Some of the basic date utility functions used throughout Clinical Reminders were optimized to get better performance.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The setting of date ranges when building a patient list from a reminder definition was made consistent with the way it is done for terms.
texts.text: The setting of date ranges when building a patient list from a reminder definition was made consistent with the way it is done for terms.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Code was added to catch problems with patient list build dates. The problems will be displayed in the list creation documentation.
texts.text: Code was added to catch problems with patient list build dates. The problems will be displayed in the list creation documentation.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The list template PXRM PATIENT LIST PATIENTS had a bottom margin of 19 and consequently it could not display two of the actions. The bottom margin was changed to 18 so these actions would display.
texts.text: The list template PXRM PATIENT LIST PATIENTS had a bottom margin of 19 and consequently it could not display two of the actions. The bottom margin was changed to 18 so these actions would display.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The display of Extract Definitions didn’t show the fields INCLUDE DECEASED PATIENTS and INCLUDE TEST PATIENTS. If the value is NULL, then the display will show “NO.”
texts.text: The display of Extract Definitions didn’t show the fields INCLUDE DECEASED PATIENTS and INCLUDE TEST PATIENTS. If the value is NULL, then the display will show “NO.”

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Changes were made so that if deceased and/or test patients are included on a patient list, they will be marked with a “D” or “T.”
texts.text: Changes were made so that if deceased and/or test patients are included on a patient list, they will be marked with a “D” or “T.”

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: When patient demographic report output was queued to p-message or a printer, the output never appeared. This was traced to incorrectly calling a Kernel queuing routine, which was corrected. Work on this also uncovered a problem in some VADPT routines that were not properly protecting variables, in particular % which is also used in the Kernel queuing routines. This may explain why some reminder report outputs have disappeared in the past. A Remedy ticket, #183747, was filed for VADPT.
texts.text: When patient demographic report output was queued to p-message or a printer, the output never appeared. This was traced to incorrectly calling a Kernel queuing routine, which was corrected. Work on this also uncovered a problem in some VADPT routines that were not properly protecting variables, in particular % which is also used in the Kernel queuing routines. This may explain why some reminder report outputs have disappeared in the past. A Remedy ticket, #183747, was filed for VADPT.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: All sequences in patient lists and extracts were converted from three-character free-text to a number between 1 and 999. Existing entries will be converted by the post-init.
texts.text: All sequences in patient lists and extracts were converted from three-character free-text to a number between 1 and 999. Existing entries will be converted by the post-init.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The display of patient list creation documentation was improved. The header was expanded to two lines so the entire name of the patient list can be seen. The list template right margin was changed to 132 so the entire display can be seen. The number of patients was moved to a separate line.
texts.text: The display of patient list creation documentation was improved. The header was expanded to two lines so the entire name of the patient list can be seen. The list template right margin was changed to 132 so the entire display can be seen. The number of patients was moved to a separate line.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The extract summary display was changed to make it easier to read.
texts.text: The extract summary display was changed to make it easier to read.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The list template PXRM PATIENT LIST is obsolete, so it was added to the build as “delete at site.”
texts.text: The list template PXRM PATIENT LIST is obsolete, so it was added to the build as “delete at site.”

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The list template PXRM PATIENT LIST USER had incorrect caption information; it was corrected.
texts.text: The list template PXRM PATIENT LIST USER had incorrect caption information; it was corrected.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Two problems that arose when running an extract against a reminder definition were repaired: 1) an undefined error when the reminder definition did not have a print name and 2) the reminder output was not stored in the correct sequence order.
texts.text: Two problems that arose when running an extract against a reminder definition were repaired: 1) an undefined error when the reminder definition did not have a print name and 2) the reminder output was not stored in the correct sequence order.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Extracts were changed to increment the patient list name created from an extract, if the extract is re-run for a previous period. The number at the end of the patient list will match the number at the end of the extract.
texts.text: Extracts were changed to increment the patient list name created from an extract, if the extract is re-run for a previous period. The number at the end of the patient list will match the number at the end of the extract.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Display of the operation was added to output for Rule Set Test.
texts.text: Display of the operation was added to output for Rule Set Test.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: It was found that the SEQUENCE fields in Reminder Extract Definitions, Reminder Extract Counting Rules, Reminder Counting Groups, and Reminder List Rule Sets did not enforce uniqueness. In other words, there was nothing to prevent creation of a Rule Set with two number 1 sequences. A key that enforces uniqueness was added to each of these fields.
texts.text: It was found that the SEQUENCE fields in Reminder Extract Definitions, Reminder Extract Counting Rules, Reminder Counting Groups, and Reminder List Rule Sets did not enforce uniqueness. In other words, there was nothing to prevent creation of a Rule Set with two number 1 sequences. A key that enforces uniqueness was added to each of these fields.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The following list rule changes were made:
texts.text: The following list rule changes were made:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: *IHD QUERI 412 DIAGNOSIS

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Reminder Reports
texts.text: Reminder Reports

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Timing data was added to the output of Reminder Due Reports.
texts.text: Timing data was added to the output of Reminder Due Reports.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The code to build location lists for reminder due reports was replaced with a call to the standard location list builder used for evaluating location list findings. This makes location list building consistent throughout the package and provides improved performance.
texts.text: The code to build location lists for reminder due reports was replaced with a call to the standard location list builder used for evaluating location list findings. This makes location list building consistent throughout the package and provides improved performance.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: A problem with historical reports including patients whose Visit date was past the end of the report ending date was corrected.
texts.text: A problem with historical reports including patients whose Visit date was past the end of the report ending date was corrected.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: After patch PXRM*2*4, the list of clinics without patients when running reports against all outpatient locations was more accurate and this caused it to be greatly expanded. To make the list more manageable, two things were done.
texts.text: After patch PXRM*2*4, the list of clinics without patients when running reports against all outpatient locations was more accurate and this caused it to be greatly expanded. To make the list more manageable, two things were done.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: First, when building the clinic list, if the clinic’s inactive date is before the beginning date of the report, the clinic will not be added to list.
texts.text: First, when building the clinic list, if the clinic’s inactive date is before the beginning date of the report, the clinic will not be added to list.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Second, a new prompt “Print locations with no patients” was added. If the answer is affirmative, the list of locations without patients will be displayed at the end of the report. This field will be stored in a reminder report template. Remedy # 168443 and #168399.
texts.text: Second, a new prompt “Print locations with no patients” was added. If the answer is affirmative, the list of locations without patients will be displayed at the end of the report. This field will be stored in a reminder report template. Remedy # 168443 and #168399.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The same help text for the Service Category field will be used when creating a new report or editing a report template. Remedy #171186.
texts.text: The same help text for the Service Category field will be used when creating a new report or editing a report template. Remedy #171186.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: An undefined error that occurred when running a detailed PCMM provider report was corrected. Remedy #175319.
texts.text: An undefined error that occurred when running a detailed PCMM provider report was corrected. Remedy #175319.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: When reports were run on a future date and the future date was the first day of the month, any patients that had visits on that date were missed. This was corrected. Remedy #169086 and #176643.
texts.text: When reports were run on a future date and the future date was the first day of the month, any patients that had visits on that date were missed. This was corrected. Remedy #169086 and #176643.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: An undefined error that occurred when saving a reminder due report output to a patient list was fixed.
texts.text: An undefined error that occurred when saving a reminder due report output to a patient list was fixed.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Due to changes made elsewhere, a call to a Scheduling API to get appointment data was no longer needed for a historical summary report so it was removed. This should speed up summary reports.
texts.text: Due to changes made elsewhere, a call to a Scheduling API to get appointment data was no longer needed for a historical summary report so it was removed. This should speed up summary reports.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Reminder Taxonomies
texts.text: Reminder Taxonomies

texts.label: text
texts.orig: CPT codes 58290-58294, 58951, and 58552-58554 were added to the national taxonomy VA-WH HYSTERECTOMY W/CERVIX REMOVED.  The CPT code 90715 was added to the VA-TETANUS DIPHTHERIA taxonomy.
texts.text: CPT codes 58290-58294, 58951, and 58552-58554 were added to the national taxonomy VA-WH HYSTERECTOMY W/CERVIX REMOVED.  The CPT code 90715 was added to the VA-TETANUS DIPHTHERIA taxonomy.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Miscellaneous
texts.text: Miscellaneous

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The option, TIU Template Reminder Dialog Parameter, was added to the CPRS Configuration menu on the Reminder Managers Menu. It lets users edit the TIU TEMPLATE REMINDER DIALOG parameter while in Reminders options rather than going through the Parameters Menu.
texts.text: The option, TIU Template Reminder Dialog Parameter, was added to the CPRS Configuration menu on the Reminder Managers Menu. It lets users edit the TIU TEMPLATE REMINDER DIALOG parameter while in Reminders options rather than going through the Parameters Menu.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: GEC
texts.text: GEC

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: As requested by the primary GEC stakeholder, several reminder dialog entries were moved from the Nursing Assessment GEC dialog to the Care Recommendation GEC dialog. A post-install routine changes several Health Factors from one GEC dialog to another.
texts.text: As requested by the primary GEC stakeholder, several reminder dialog entries were moved from the Nursing Assessment GEC dialog to the Care Recommendation GEC dialog. A post-install routine changes several Health Factors from one GEC dialog to another.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Summary of Mental Health Instruments in Reminder Dialogs
texts.text: Summary of Mental Health Instruments in Reminder Dialogs

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Instruments with free-text responses can be administered in reminder dialogs once CPRS v27 is released (won’t work in CPRS v26)
texts.text: Instruments with free-text responses can be administered in reminder dialogs once CPRS v27 is released (won’t work in CPRS v26)

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: With CPRS v27, pop-up messages can be added to provide directions based on result of MH instrument; for example, if PHQ-2 is positive, you need to complete a PHQ-9 now
texts.text: With CPRS v27, pop-up messages can be added to provide directions based on result of MH instrument; for example, if PHQ-2 is positive, you need to complete a PHQ-9 now

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Clinical reminders includes a parameter that determines the length of a test that can be administered through reminder dialogs—defaults to 35 items
texts.text: Clinical reminders includes a parameter that determines the length of a test that can be administered through reminder dialogs—defaults to 35 items

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Tests in reminder dialogs will follow restrictions set with MH security keys
texts.text: Tests in reminder dialogs will follow restrictions set with MH security keys

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Tests that are restricted or require a license won’t display questions/responses in progress note text
texts.text: Tests that are restricted or require a license won’t display questions/responses in progress note text

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Completing long tests through reminder dialogs is not recommended
texts.text: Completing long tests through reminder dialogs is not recommended

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Patient-administered testing should be completed using the Secure Desktop functionality in MHA.
texts.text: Patient-administered testing should be completed using the Secure Desktop functionality in MHA.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: How does MHA help Integrated Care?
texts.text: How does MHA help Integrated Care?

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Need Outcomes, using standardized tools, such as PHQ9
texts.text: Need Outcomes, using standardized tools, such as PHQ9

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Can begin using MHA to access and record assessment data for veterans in integrated care programs
texts.text: Can begin using MHA to access and record assessment data for veterans in integrated care programs

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Can use MHA and clinical reminders to identify those patients in a “watchful waiting” phase, needing regular assessment
texts.text: Can use MHA and clinical reminders to identify those patients in a “watchful waiting” phase, needing regular assessment

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: How many veterans in integrated care clinics over the age of 65 endorsed suicidal ideation on PHQ9?
texts.text: How many veterans in integrated care clinics over the age of 65 endorsed suicidal ideation on PHQ9?

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Which veterans presenting with cardiac complaints have positive scores on Beck Anxiety Scale?
texts.text: Which veterans presenting with cardiac complaints have positive scores on Beck Anxiety Scale?

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: What is the severity of depression seen in veterans enrolled in the pain clinic?
texts.text: What is the severity of depression seen in veterans enrolled in the pain clinic?

texts.label: text
texts.orig: For Secure Patient Testing
texts.text: For Secure Patient Testing

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Patients can complete testing on computer, using Secure Desktop functionality
texts.text: Patients can complete testing on computer, using Secure Desktop functionality

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Logs computer off network when testing is completed.  No other application can be accessed
texts.text: Logs computer off network when testing is completed.  No other application can be accessed

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Secure Desktop must be installed on the PC (not run from server)
texts.text: Secure Desktop must be installed on the PC (not run from server)

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Secure Desktop should only be installed on PC’s that veterans will use to complete assessments in MHA
texts.text: Secure Desktop should only be installed on PC’s that veterans will use to complete assessments in MHA
